mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is invalid if rate cuts do not coincide with higher forward returns.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in more than 55% of periods with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An above-trend CPI inflation print forces expectations of tighter policy and higher real discount rates, which should pressure equity multiples and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when inflation is accelerating versus its prior 3-month average.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 3-month moving average by more than 0.4%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes deliver positive 20d returns in more than 60% of cases, or if adding the inflation trigger does not improve hit rate versus unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate indicates weakening household income and demand, which should compress earnings expectations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal should work when unemployment is trending up, not when it is flat.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward returns are not negative in a majority of UNRATE uptrends, or if the effect disappears after excluding one-off recession months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in payroll growth and retail sales signals stronger final demand and operating leverage, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism depends on both labor income and consumer spending improving together, not either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-RSAFS signal does not outperform either single-series signal, or if positive 60d returns occur in less than 55% of joint-improvement episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Stronger industrial production relative to recent history signals improving real activity and operating leverage in cyclical sectors, which should push S&P 500 returns higher over the next 5 trading days. The signal is a short-horizon continuation effect from better production data.
failure_condition: Reject if the 5d forward return after positive INDPRO surprises is not above baseline, or if the rolling 1-year hit rate falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts reflects tightening credit, weaker construction demand, and early-cycle slowdown pressure, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is strongest when housing starts are falling at the same time as employment growth slows.
failure_condition: Reject if 60d forward returns are not negative in at least 55% of housing-decline episodes, or if the signal vanishes after controlling for broad market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate combined with stable payroll growth implies a resilient labor market that supports consumption and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is regime-dependent because it works best when unemployment is already low and not rising sharply.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-UNRATE and stable-PAYEMS condition does not raise the probability of positive 20d returns above 55%, or if either proxy alone explains the same effect.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is above target and the policy rate is elevated, equity valuations should face a double headwind from tighter real rates and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if high rates coincide with decelerating inflation and the market instead prices a soft landing.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and CPIAUCSL is rising on a 3-month annualized basis above 3.0%
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate/high-inflation episodes do not produce negative 60d returns more often than not, or if the sign flips positive when the policy rate remains restrictive.
